senses.definition: an award made of gold or gold-colored metal, given to the winner of a race or competition to symbolize their victory

examples.x: He trained for years to earn a gold medal.
The athlete received a gold medal for first place.
The gold medal was awarded to the fastest runner.
The swimmer took home three gold medals.
She wore her gold medal during the victory ceremony.

senses.definition: a recognition awarded to the second-place finisher in a competition or sporting event
a recognition awarded to the third-place finisher in a competition or sporting event
a structure used in sports competitions consisting of three adjacent platforms of different levels, on which winners stand to receive their awards

examples.x: During the conference, each presenter had a designated time slot to speak from the podium.
After winning third place, he proudly stood on the lowest step of the podium to receive his medal.

senses.definition: a flat piece of metal, typically of the size and shape of a large coin, given to the winner of a competition or to someone who has done an act of bravery in war, etc.

examples.x: Every participant received a medal at the end of the race.
He's aiming for a medal in the next Olympics.
She keeps all her medals in a special case.

senses.definition: an object that is awarded to the winner of a competition

examples.x: The team lifted the championship trophy after their victory.
He proudly displayed the trophy on his shelf.
She won a golden trophy for her excellent performance.
The trophy was engraved with the winner’s name.
The athlete trained hard to bring home the trophy.

senses.definition: a board that displays the score in a contest or game

examples.x: The scoreboard displayed the team's points during the basketball game.
The scoreboard updated automatically after each round of play.
He glanced at the scoreboard to see how much time was left in the match.
The scoreboard lit up with fireworks when a player scored.
They took a picture of the scoreboard to remember the exciting finish.

senses.definition: the best performance or result, or the highest or lowest level that has ever been reached, especially in sport

examples.x: The gymnast achieved a perfect score, setting a new record in the floor exercise event.
The soccer team's undefeated streak broke the league record for the longest winning streak.
The weightlifter lifted an astonishing amount of weight, breaking the national record in his weight class.
Her marathon time set a new record for the fastest female runner in the race's history.

senses.definition: a trophy awarded to the winner of a tournament or league

examples.x: He was excited to add the bowling cup to his collection of trophies.
The coach's strategy helped the baseball team win the state cup.
The runners pushed their limits to win the marathon cup.
Their chess team was determined to bring home the regional cup.

senses.definition: a second-place result in a competition or event

examples.x: She was proud of her runner-up finish in the marathon.
The team celebrated their runner-up finish in the championship.
The cyclist achieved a runner-up finish in the race.
Despite the loss, the runner-up finish was a great achievement for the young athlete.
His runner-up finish in the tennis tournament was a career highlight.

senses.definition: the numerical position or order of teams and individuals based on their performance in a competition or over a period of time

examples.x: His consistent wins earned him a high ranking in the tennis tournament.
The team's ranking improved after their victory in the last match.
She checked her ranking on the leaderboard after each event.
The player's ranking dropped after a series of losses.

senses.definition: a marking or line indicating the end of a race or competition

examples.x: The runner crossed the finish line first.
She sprinted toward the finish line with determination.
The crowd cheered as he approached the finish line.

senses.definition: an athlete or horse that excels when maintaining the lead position in a race

examples.x: The race favored front runners due to the clear weather conditions.
The coach advised him to be a front runner and start strong.
He prefers to be a front runner to avoid the crowded pack.
The jockey knew his horse was a front runner and pushed for an early lead.
The front runner maintained a steady lead throughout the race.

senses.definition: (in some sports) an occasion in which a player manages to score three times in a single game of soccer, hockey, etc.
a belt that signifies a high rank or level of proficiency achieved through training and testing in martial arts

examples.x: He earned his black belt in karate after years of dedicated practice.
She is a skilled martial artist with a third-degree black belt.
The dojo held a ceremony to award black belts to their students.
The school offers advanced training for those aiming to earn a black belt.
They celebrated their son's achievement of earning a junior black belt.

senses.definition: a race in which two or more competitors finish at the exact same time

examples.x: The race ended in a dead heat, with both runners crossing the finish line at the same time.
The two teams were so evenly matched that they finished in a dead heat.
The final score was a dead heat, leaving the judges to decide the winner based on other factors.
The championship game ended in a dead heat, and both teams were awarded the title.
The contest was a dead heat, and no one could believe how close it had been.

senses.definition: to gain a point, goal, etc. in a game, competition, or sport

examples.x: He scored a goal in the final minute.
Maria scored three points with that move.
Our team scored twice during the second half.
They tried hard but couldn't score against the defense.
During the match, both players scored multiple times.

senses.definition: the amount of prize money or earnings awarded to winners or participants in a competition

examples.x: The winner of the golf tournament took home a large purse.
She earned a significant purse for finishing first in the marathon.
The boxer fought hard to claim the purse in the championship match.
Athletes compete fiercely for the purse in track and field events.
He donated a portion of his purse to charity after winning the race.

senses.definition: a result of a race that competitors are extremely close, requiring photographic evidence to determine the winner

examples.x: The runners crossed the finish line so closely that it required a photo finish to determine the winner.
The swimming competition ended in a thrilling photo finish.
Spectators held their breath as they awaited the photo finish results of the cycling race.
He won the 100-meter dash in a spectacular photo finish.
The Olympic marathon ended in a dramatic photo finish with three runners neck and neck.

senses.definition: to win against someone in a war, game, contest, etc.

examples.x: The army worked together to defeat the enemy in a decisive battle.
The chess champion strategically moved to defeat their opponent and secure victory.
Athletes train rigorously to defeat their competitors and achieve success in the competition.
In the debate, she eloquently defeated her opponent with well-reasoned arguments.
Teams relentlessly competed, and one eventually defeated the other to advance.

senses.definition: (in games and sports) a disadvantage that a team or player is given for violating a rule
a rule violation where a player is positioned improperly, usually ahead of the ball or puck, in a way that is not allowed

examples.x: The referee called an offside during the soccer match.
She was flagged for an offside just before she scored the goal.
The team practiced avoiding offsides in training.
The offside was crucial in maintaining fair play in the hockey game.
They lost a chance to score because of an offside.

senses.definition: to step onto the playing area, like a field or stage, especially in sports or performances

examples.x: The soccer team went out onto the field with high spirits.
The basketball players went out onto the court to warm up.
The orchestra members went out on stage and played beautiful music.
The players went out onto the ice for the ice hockey match.

senses.definition: an individual, team, etc. who is regarded as weaker compared to others and has little chance of success as a result

examples.x: The underdog team surprised everyone by defeating the reigning champions in the final match.
She always roots for the underdog in sports because she loves a good comeback story.
The underdog candidate won the election against all odds.
Despite being the underdog in the negotiation, he managed to secure a favorable deal for his company.
The underdog film, with its low budget and unknown actors, became a surprise box office hit.

senses.definition: the rank of a team or individual in a sport competition

examples.x: The team’s current standing in the league is second place.
His strong performance improved his standing in the tournament.
She checked her standing after each race to see if she qualified for the finals.
The athlete’s consistent wins have kept her at the top of the standings.
Their win today could boost their standing in the championship.

senses.definition: a trophy awarded to the winner of a championship, typically in combat sports like boxing and wrestling

examples.x: He proudly wore the championship belt after his victory.
He posed for photos with his new championship belt.
She aimed to reclaim the championship belt in the rematch.
He lost the championship belt in a close match.
She defended her championship belt successfully.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt indicating basic proficiency

examples.x: She earned her yellow belt in karate.
The yellow belt signifies a beginner rank.
He proudly wore his yellow belt to class.
The instructor awarded him a yellow belt.
He recently received a yellow belt and is proud of himself.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt that signifies an advanced level of proficiency, just below the black belt

examples.x: After passing the test, she was awarded her brown belt.
His goal is to advance from brown belt to black belt.
She practiced hard to achieve her brown belt.
The brown belt represents a high level of skill.
Earning a brown belt requires dedication and discipline.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt that signifies an intermediate level of proficiency and skill development

examples.x: She wore her purple belt proudly at the martial arts competition.
Practitioners with purple belt are expected to mentor lower belts.
He trains hard to advance from purple belt to brown belt.
He is proud to earn a purple belt in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.
She earned her purple belt after three years of training.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt signifying an intermediate level of proficiency that is just below the purple belt

examples.x: She proudly displayed her new blue belt, freshly earned from the dojo.
The blue belt symbolized his progression in martial arts training.
She carefully folded her blue belt after a rigorous training session.
The dojo awarded custom-made blue belts to commemorate their achievements.
He tied his gi with a sturdy blue belt before heading to class.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt that represents a level of proficiency above beginner, often indicating intermediate skills and knowledge

examples.x: She proudly wore her new green belt to class.
The green belt signifies his progress in martial arts training.
He proudly displayed his green belt during the ceremony.
She earned her green belt after passing a difficult test.
His goal is to advance from a green belt to a blue belt.

senses.definition: (martial arts) a belt that signifies a beginner level of proficiency, above white and yellow belts

examples.x: He earned his orange belt after passing the test.
She tied her orange belt tightly before starting the class.
His goal is to advance from orange belt to green belt.
The dojo held a ceremony to award orange belts to new students.
He remembered the day he received his orange belt fondly.

senses.definition: a belt that signifies a beginner's rank in martial arts, indicating the starting level of proficiency

examples.x: She kept her white belt as a reminder of her martial arts journey.
The instructor handed her a new white belt after the registration.
He tied his white belt in a neat knot, ready for practice.
She carefully folded her white belt after each training session.
He placed his white belt in his gym bag after a long day of training.

senses.definition: the act of successfully putting the ball into the opponent's goal to score points

examples.x: The team's goalscoring has improved dramatically.
She received an award for her outstanding goalscoring.

senses.definition: a rule where the first goal scored in extra time immediately ends the game and determines the winner

examples.x: The match ended with a dramatic golden goal.
He scored the golden goal in extra time.
The tournament was decided by a golden goal.
A golden goal ended the game in the first minute of extra time.
The team's golden goal secured their place in the finals.

senses.definition: a card raised by the referee that removes a player from the game

examples.x: They played with a man down after the red card.
The team struggled to recover after the early red card.
The player's deliberate handball resulted in a red card.
She received a red card for arguing with the official.
The coach benched him for the next match following the red card.

senses.definition: a warning card issued to a player for a less serious offense in sports like soccer

examples.x: The referee gave him a yellow card for the foul.
She received a yellow card for delaying the game.
His dangerous play resulted in a yellow card.
The team protested the referee's yellow card decision.
The player was cautious after getting a yellow card.

senses.definition: the championship trophy awarded annually to the National Hockey League playoff winner

examples.x: The Stanley Cup is the ultimate prize in professional hockey.
Fans eagerly follow their team's journey to the Stanley Cup.
Each player gets to spend a day with the Stanley Cup after winning.
The Stanley Cup playoffs are watched by millions of fans.

senses.definition: to achieve the best result in something, particularly in sports, competition, etc.
not ranking, indicating a participant did not finish in a top position or win a prize

examples.x: The unplaced runner finished just outside the top three in the marathon.
Despite her best efforts, she remained unplaced in the swimming competition.
The unplaced team struggled to score throughout the tournament.
He was disappointed to be unplaced after leading for much of the race.
Several unplaced candidates withdrew from the competition early on.

senses.definition: a score of fifty points or goals

examples.x: She celebrated her basketball career's half-century with a stunning performance last night.
The swimmer's win marked her half-century of victories in national competitions.
His half-century of career touchdowns places him among the league's all-time greats.
The athlete's half-century of points in a single game set a new school record.
The marathon runner crossed the finish line to achieve his half-century of completed races.

senses.definition: a decisive victory in which a team or player achieves consecutive wins in any game, contest, or other similar events
(in sports) to be unable to get a win against the opposing team

examples.x: It's a long time since we dropped a game.
She dropped the second set but came back to win the match.
They didn't drop any points until halfway through the season.

senses.definition: to occupy or hold a specific rank or standing in a race or competition
